sanskrit: परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् । धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय संभवामि युगे युगे ॥
hindiTranslation: साधुओंभक्तों की रक्षा करनेके लिये, पापकर्म करनेवालोंका विनाश करनेके लिये और धर्मकी भलीभाँति स्थापना करनेके लिये मैं युगयुगमें प्रकट हुआ करता हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: For the protection of the righteous, for the destruction of wrongdoers, and for the establishment of dharma, I appear age after age.
essence: The divine returns whenever order must be restored.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he appears again and again to protect the good, stop wrongdoing, and restore dharma.
simpleInsight: There is comfort in knowing order is not abandoned. What is worth protecting is protected.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen and Krishna is revealing why divine presence enters the world. After saying he appears whenever dharma declines, Krishna adds the purpose of that appearance: to protect the righteous, remove destructive forces, and restore right order.
modernRelevance: When a family system is poisoned by one cruel person, or a workplace has become toxic, you want to believe repair is still possible. This verse insists that protection and restoration are not fantasies.
translationLiteral: परित्राणाय (for protection) / साधूनाम् (of the righteous) / विनाशाय (for destruction) / च (and) / दुष्कृताम् (of wrongdoers) / धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय (for the purpose of establishing dharma) / संभवामि (I appear) / युगे युगे (age after age)
insight: It shifts the mind from passive despair to trust in restorative action.
